name: Thumbprints
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Mix together the butter and brown sugar in a bowl until well combined.
3. Add the egg white and vanilla; beat until fluffy.
4. Gradually add flour and salt, stirring until the dough holds together.
5. Form the dough into 1-inch balls.
6. Beat the remaining egg white slightly.
7. Dip each ball of dough into the beaten egg white, then roll in walnuts.
8. Place the thumbprint-shaped cookies about 2 inches apart on an ungreased baking sheet.
9. Press your thumb gently into the center of each cookie to create a slight indentation.
10. Bake for 10 minutes or until lightly browned.
11. Remove from the oven immediately and let cool on the baking sheet.
12. Fill each thumbprint with red currant jelly (4 centers) and black currant jelly (4 centers).
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
